what_it_does: Maintains a durable backlog of active campaigns, queued work, follow-ons, gated decisions, verification notes, and context pointers for the counterpart-design project loop.
observations: The file begins with an explicit operating law: capture future work immediately, point to context, and avoid treating chat as storage.
It organizes work by horizons, with HORIZON 0 tied to loop completion criteria and later sections holding Tim-directed or deferred work.
Many entries include dated provenance, Tim quotes, status tags such as DONE, OPEN, BLOCKED, GATED, and references to campaign names or loop identifiers.

The shown content is heavily focused on rule-core, spacing circuits, mobile viewer redesign, WORK|PANEL behavior, live composition, graph legibility, substrate regeneration, and DNA.control migration.
Entries frequently record what was verified, what could not be verified in the sandbox, and what needs Tim's device or decision.
standouts: The backlog is both a task ledger and a reconstruction map for future sessions.
The mobile WORK|PANEL sequence is recorded as a long decision-and-implementation history with remaining refinements attached to specific prior passes.
The live-composed work slide entry captures a central unresolved architectural direction: replacing static mockups with derived, controllable work surfaces.
conventions: Uses horizon headings, bold task labels, dated notes, status markers, and inline context pointers.
Prefers provenance-heavy entries that preserve the reasoning trail alongside the task.
Uses struck-through completed items and checkmarks for landed work.
Breaks its stated pointer-not-copy convention in places by embedding extensive analysis and implementation detail directly in the backlog.
concerns: The file is very long and dense for a backlog, making scanning and prioritization difficult from this file alone.
Several completed or superseded items remain near active sections, which may obscure the current actionable set.
Some entries mix task, diagnosis, proof, design rationale, and historical correction in one bullet.
The shown HORIZON 0 section contains repeated completed criteria lines and stale-looking active labels after the deep-rebuild is marked complete.
notes: Brief someone on this as the project’s autonomous memory ledger: it preserves what to do next, why it exists, who decided it, and where to resume.
questions: From this file alone, which open item should be taken next when multiple Tim-gated and autonomous candidates are present?
Which entries should move to a landed log versus remain in active backlog after completion?
What is the intended maximum detail level for an entry before it should point to a separate analysis file instead?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual role as durable future-work storage, but the file often stores detailed analysis instead of only pointers.
intention_signals: It seems intended to let any future session reconstruct the project’s open work, constraints, decisions, and verification state without relying on chat memory.
novelty: Unusually blends backlog, decision record, verification ledger, and design intent map in one operational Markdown file.
contribution: Contributes a time-ordered, provenance-rich work ledger that preserves deferred tasks, decisions, blockers, verification status, and context pointers for continuing the system without losing project intent.
summary_for_embedding: A durable project backlog for counterpart-design that records horizons, active campaigns, completed passes, deferred follow-ons, Tim decisions, verification notes, blockers, and context pointers.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to keep the development loop autonomous by capturing every future task and reminder with enough context to resume later.
